出.origin: A little sprout 屮 pushing up out of its container 凵 — popping out to go outside!

sentence.en: Mom went out to buy groceries.

入.origin: Like an arrow pointing the way inside — step in and enter the door.

sentence.en: Everyone please take your seats.

座.origin: Under a wide roof 广, two people sit 坐 down — that spot is your seat.

sentence.en: My seat is in the front.

立.origin: A person standing tall with feet planted firmly on the ground 一.

sentence.en: He stood at the door waiting for me.

地.origin: Soil 土 stretches everywhere — the wide, solid ground under your feet.

sentence.en: The kitten lies on the ground sleeping.

父.origin: A strong hand holding a tool — the dad who works hard for the family.

sentence.en: Father likes to read the newspaper.

母.origin: A picture of a caring mother — the dots remind us she feeds her baby.

sentence.en: The food Mother makes is delicious.

电.origin: Picture lightning flashing from a storm cloud — that crackling power is electricity!

sentence.en: At night we turn on the electric light.

禾.origin: A grain plant with its heavy head bending over — ripe rice ready to pick.

sentence.en: The rice seedlings in the field are bright green.

金.origin: Shiny bits of metal hidden in the earth — precious, gleaming gold.

sentence.en: This stone looks like gold.

衣.origin: A picture of a flowing robe with sleeves — clothes to keep you warm.

sentence.en: It's cold, wear more clothes.

车.origin: Look down at a cart and you see its wheels and body — a vehicle that rolls!

sentence.en: Dad drives a car to work.

瓜.origin: Curling vines with a round melon hanging at the bottom — yummy and juicy.

sentence.en: Eating watermelon in summer is so sweet.

生.origin: A young shoot 屮 sprouting from the soil 土 — new life growing up!

sentence.en: Today is my birthday.

叫.origin: A mouth 口 opening wide to call out loud — shout and someone will hear!

sentence.en: Mom calls me home to eat.

岁.origin: A mountain 山 plus evening 夕 — as days and seasons pass, you grow a year older.

sentence.en: Little brother is five years old this year.

喜.origin: Beating a happy drum 壴 while a mouth 口 sings — that joyful feeling is happiness!

sentence.en: I like to draw.

欢.origin: A bright, cheerful feeling — when your heart is glad and you want to smile.

sentence.en: The children laugh joyfully while playing games.

习.origin: Picture a little bird flapping its wings over and over — that's how you practice and learn.

sentence.en: We study Chinese characters every day.

的.origin: A tiny helper word that joins things together, like "mine" or "the cat's tail."

sentence.en: This is my book.

开.origin: Two hands 廾 lifting the latch 一 off a door — push it open!

sentence.en: Please open the window.

真.origin: Eyes 目 looking carefully to see what is straight and real — truly true!

sentence.en: This story is real.

兴.origin: Hands lifting up together with joy — everyone excited and full of energy!

sentence.en: I am very happy to see you.
